items.name: ARPA-E HORNIG (High-performance Optimized Recycled Nuclear Isotopes for Gen IV reactors)
items.provides: Cost-shared R&D funding for TRU fuel design, fabrication, testing, modeling and qualification
items.deadline_label: Concept paper deadline (passed); full-application phase is by invitation only, dates not yet published — confirm current phase on ARPA-E eXCHANGE
items.fit: Very high — direct match to newcleo's MOX/transuranic fuel and lead-cooled fast reactor fuel-qualification work
items.funds: Up to $50 million to develop transuranic (TRU) fuels for advanced reactors using materials recovered from used nuclear fuel and other strategic reserves; individual awards ranging from $500K–$7M
items.note: Program metrics target a domestic TRU fuel supply chain, a levelized cost of fuel of 1¢/kWh, and TRU fuel qualification within seven years. HORNIG will support design, fabrication, testing and qualification of TRU-based fuel systems, aiming to shorten qualification timelines from decades to under seven years. Concept-paper window has closed for this round; watch for a possible second round or full-application NOFO.

items.name: GAIN Nuclear Energy (NE) Vouchers — FY-2026 4th Round
items.sponsor: DOE Office of Nuclear Energy / Gateway for Accelerated Innovation in Nuclear (GAIN), administered by INL
items.provides: Access to national laboratory technical capabilities and expertise (e.g., materials qualification, testing) at no cost to the recipient
items.deadline_label: FY-2026 4th Round proposals due 5:00pm EST Friday, July 31, 2026
items.fit: High — a low-friction route for newcleo Americas to access national-lab capabilities for lead-cooled materials qualification or MOX-related testing
items.funds: Voucher recipients do not receive direct financial awards; the vouchers provide access to national laboratory capabilities at no cost to the voucher recipients
items.note: GAIN strongly recommends prospective applicants consult with GAIN staff on scope and cost estimate before applying. Requires a CRADA with the host national lab; subsequent FY26 rounds may follow this one on a similar cadence.

items.name: NRIC Nuclear Energy Launch Pad (Launch Pad INL / Launch Pad USA)
items.sponsor: DOE / National Reactor Innovation Center (NRIC) at Idaho National Laboratory
items.kind: DOE authorization + site-access pathway (Request for Applications), rolling
items.provides: DOE Atomic Energy Act authorization (bypassing initial NRC licensing), pre-characterized federal land/site access, and technical assistance for reactor, fuel-fabrication, recycling or enrichment facilities; no cash — applicant funds all design, construction, operation and decommissioning
items.deadline_label: Initial-round submission deadline July 8, 2026 (5pm MDT); RFA remains open on a continual rolling basis thereafter, with further selections announced periodically
items.fit: High — covers fuel-fabrication and recycling facilities explicitly, and offers an alternative/complementary DOE authorization route to newcleo's Savannah River fuel-fab and LFR ambitions
items.funds: No direct funding; site parcels, DOE authorization pathway, and technical/regulatory assistance only
items.note: NRIC and DOE are establishing the Launch Pad to promote rapid private-industry deployment of advanced nuclear technologies, building on the Reactor and Fuel Line Pilot Programs. The RFA invites developers of reactors, enrichment facilities, fuel fabrication plants, and reprocessing/recycling operations to apply. Foreign-ownership review (relevant given newcleo Americas' European parent) can extend timelines — applicants have asked NRIC to clarify whether Strategic Partnership Project/OTA foreign-ownership review can begin in parallel with selection.

items.name: Surplus Plutonium Utilization Program
items.sponsor: U.S. DOE Office of Nuclear Energy / NNSA (Idaho Operations Office), RFA DE-FOA-0003594
items.kind: Material-allocation program using Other Transaction Agreements (OTA), not a grant
items.provides: Access to DOE-owned surplus weapons-grade plutonium as fuel feedstock plus a DOE authorization pathway that can fast-track future NRC licensing; no cash to the applicant
items.deadline_label: Applications closed (extended to Dec 1, 2025); program now in advanced OTA negotiations with selected companies — no new deadline published
items.fit: Direct — newcleo is named as the fuel-cycle/capital partner in Oklo's selection; this is the specific mechanism behind newcleo's Savannah River MOX strategy
items.funds: About 15.3 metric tons of plutonium in oxide form and about 4.4 metric tons in metal form made available as feedstock; DOE has signaled it may also seek partial cost recovery for the material rather than providing cash
items.note: Oklo and Flibe Energy were selected by DOE for advanced negotiations under the program, alongside Exodys Energy, Shine Technologies and Standard Nuclear. Oklo would lead utilisation of the plutonium while newcleo brings fuel experience and potential project capital, framed as a disposition-through-use pathway. Selected companies must meet all costs of carrying out their proposal. As a European-owned entity, newcleo's role is via Oklo's OTA and subject to US security/safeguards review — confirm final agreement terms before treating this as committed.

items.name: DOE Fuel Line Pilot Program
items.sponsor: U.S. DOE (Office of Nuclear Energy), authorization-based RFA
items.kind: DOE authorization pathway for advanced nuclear fuel fabrication lines (non-NRC)
items.provides: DOE authorization to build/operate a fuel fabrication line outside national laboratories, intended to unlock private funding and fast-track future NRC licensing; no cash
items.deadline_label: No fixed deadline confirmed; DOE has run at least two selection rounds (2025 and mid-2026) and continues to make selections as capacity allows
items.fit: Medium-high — Oklo (newcleo's Savannah River MOX partner) has already used this exact mechanism for fuel-fabrication facilities, illustrating the pathway newcleo Americas could also use for its own facility
items.funds: No direct funding; each company is responsible for all costs associated with construction, operation, and decommissioning of their fuel fabrication facilities, and must manage sourcing of nuclear material feedstock
items.note: Oklo was selected to build and operate three fuel fabrication facilities to support its Aurora and Pluto reactors, and possibly other fast reactors. Not yet confirmed whether a new open call exists for additional entrants beyond the two rounds already run.

items.name: Advanced Reactor Demonstration Program (ARDP) — Pathway 1
items.sponsor: U.S. DOE Office of Nuclear Energy
items.kind: Cost-shared demonstration cooperative agreement (fully committed, reference only)
items.provides: Reference only — cost-shared capital already committed to two named awardees; not an open or rolling competition
items.deadline_label: No open call; awards made in 2020 and fully committed
items.fit: Low direct fit for newcleo (awardees use sodium-cooled and HTGR designs, not lead-cooled/MOX) — included only for completeness of the federal nuclear-demonstration funding map
items.funds: DOE awarded TerraPower and X-energy $80 million each in initial funding, with a total planned investment of $3.2 billion over seven years, matched by industry cost-share
items.note: Included as a reference/allocated item per brief instructions to keep the capital map complete; no action item for newcleo.
